orpAssembly.command: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 848 | convergence assembly | Explain the 282 First-Failure Mechanism" --json

frontierComparison.interpretation: The open ledger count increased by recording the bucket-boundary object, but the 30-bucket token was consumed into 19 terminal closures, 10 exact partial two-root bucket boundaries, and one exact q109 nonuniform boundary. This is progress only if the next step recombines or further partitions the nonuniform/partial tokens rather than descending into singleton q children.

assembledPieces.claim: The p443 availability split exposed the selected q97 square-obstruction child and left the p443-unavailable complement open.
assembledPieces.boundary: It does not close the p443-unavailable complement or the wider p4217 complement.

assembledPieces.claim: The p151 route is ledgered as blocked/open, and the p479 reserve is exact-certified as a squarefree representative repair before any deeper selector descent.
assembledPieces.boundary: Representative repair is not subclass coverage.

assembledPieces.claim: The selected q97 child is partitioned by the p479 availability formula into 1140 available and 228301 unavailable residues modulo 479^2.
assembledPieces.boundary: The split itself does not close the available residues, unavailable complement, q97 child, or p4217 complement.

assembledPieces.claim: All 1140 p479-available residue classes are classified by first square-obstruction children with no survivor residues, using obstruction primes up to q=173.
assembledPieces.boundary: The square-obstruction children are not individually closed by the bulk cover.

assembledPieces.claim: The 30 obstruction-prime buckets split into 19 terminal full-family buckets covering 422 rows, 10 partial two-root buckets covering 367 rows, and one q109 nonuniform bucket covering 351 rows.
assembledPieces.boundary: Only the 19 terminal buckets are terminally closed; q109, the 10 partial buckets, their q-avoiding complements, and all upstream complements remain open.

jointTheoremClaimSupported.statement: For the selected p443/q97/p479-available branch, every recorded residue row is either terminally square-obstructed by a full-family q^2 bucket, assigned to an exact two-root partial bucket boundary, or assigned to the exact q109 nonuniform bucket boundary.

nextFiniteToken.rankDecreaseIfCompleted: The sole nonuniform bucket blocking a uniform partial-bucket theorem is either compressed into structural subfamilies or replaced by exact subbucket boundaries before any singleton q-child descent.

compressionCandidate.name: p479_q109_nonuniform_bucket_structure_packet
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All 351 p479-available residue rows whose first obstruction bucket is q109 inside the selected p443/q97 branch.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: q109 is the only bucket that did not fit the terminal/full-family or invertible two-root dichotomy. Classifying it first removes the unique nonuniform obstruction to a clean terminal/partial recombination theorem.
compressionCandidate.whyNotQ109SingletonDescent: The handoff covers the whole q109 bucket as a finite structural family. It is not a repair descent into one q109 root child or a new endpoint selector ladder.
compressionCandidate.whyBeforePartialQAvoidingCover: The partial q-avoiding complement has 5564177 descendant classes across 10 buckets. The q109 bucket is a much smaller 351-row structural blocker whose classification may expose the correct shared invariant before attacking the larger q-avoiding cover.

oneNextAction.action: Classify the whole q109 nonuniform bucket by its row-shape invariants and either prove a shared structural bucket theorem or emit exact subbucket boundaries with the deterministic successor denominator.
oneNextAction.coveredFamily: The complete q109 nonuniform bucket: 351 p479-available residue rows from the selected p443/q97 branch, not a singleton q109 child.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If no shared q109 structure is found, emit a deterministic q109 subbucket-boundary packet listing root-count/gcd/invertibility profiles, row counts, and the exact next denominator or blocker for each subbucket.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The q109 bucket is finite, already isolated, and is the sole nonuniform blocker. A singleton q109 or q127 child descent would reopen the sibling-ladder anti-pattern while leaving the bucket theorem surface unassembled.
oneNextAction.completionRule: A q109 structure packet consumes the nonuniform bucket token by proving a shared bucket theorem or by emitting exact subbucket boundaries; it must not claim p479-available, p479-unavailable, q97, p443-unavailable, p4217, or all-n closure.

coversPrimaryNextAction.reason: This assembly is specifically for the current post-bucket-boundary convergence primary action after the p479 obstruction bucket token was compressed.

nextTheoremMove: Classify the whole q109 nonuniform bucket into a shared structural theorem or exact subbucket boundaries before any q109/q127 singleton descent.
proofBoundary: This convergence assembly records the post-bucket-boundary frontier and selects q109 whole-bucket structural classification. It closes only the 19 terminal full-family bucket boundaries already proved by the bucket packet. It does not close q109, the 10 partial buckets, their q-avoiding complements, the p479-unavailable complement, the q97 child, the p443-unavailable complement, the wider p4217 complement, prove collision-free matching, or decide Problem 848.
